paragraphs.body: Over time people have always considered twins to have some sort of special bond that nobody else gets and is unique to every set of twins. Nobody can understand the science behind this bond, and it is generally understood as something special. People have created theories about why twins might have such a special and deep bond. According to Pelaez, “Right after birth, an attachment becomes established between the infant and a particular individual. According to John Bowlby’s Attachment Theory, this attachment is based on the infant’s need for safety and security.” In other words, when an infant first comes out of the womb, they immediately bond with someone; for most infants, that person is their mother. However, twins have developed this bond since they were in the womb with each other. It has been seen through the use of ultrasounds people have been able to watch the connection, attachment, and bond form between twins as early as 15 weeks along in the pregnancy. This is seen by twins physically touching and embracing each other while sharing the womb. This theory is not the only theory out there that tries and explain why twins have such a powerful bond with each other. In her article, Pelaez describes the Kinship Genetic Theory as “prosocial behaviors are predetermined by genetic relatedness.” This concludes that if the twins are identical then they have a greater and stronger bond between them compared to if they were fraternal twins due to the amount of genetic information they share. The theory also judges that the closeness and the strength of the bond that twins have are determined by gender, genes, and the shared experiences that the two individuals have shared. The article talks about possible ideas and theories that could describe why twins have such an impactful bond with each other.

annotations.text: This concludes that if the twins are identical then they have a greater and stronger bond between them compared to if they were fraternal twins due to the amount of genetic information they share.
It has been seen through the use of ultrasounds people have been able to watch the connection, attachment, and bond form between twins as early as 15 weeks along in the pregnancy.
annotations.comment: Can you write this sentence more simply and clearly?
Who is "Pelaez"? Don't forget to introduce the article and author you're summarizing.

paragraphs.body: The twin bond is different for each set of twins and it depends on how they were raised and how much of life they go through together. Going through life with my twin as my built-in best friend has been amazing and I would never change it for anything else in the world. It is the only thing I know, and I know that if I lose any of the incredible benefits that come with being a twin I would crumble up. For me personally the bond that I share with Claire is something that I know I will cherish for the rest of my life. We have grown up with each other and learned so much together. We have gone through huge milestones together and have gone through tragedies together and have come out the other side of it stronger than we went in it. We have always had each other's backs no matter if we think that the other one is wrong it does not matter. The only thing to me in those moments is that I am backing her up and helping her in any way that I can.

annotations.text: Going through life with my twin as my built-in best friend has been amazing and I would never change it for anything else in the world. It is

paragraphs.body: Some of my favorite memories with her are when we just talk about whatever new thing we are currently obsessed with because it changes quite frequently. I love finding new artists, television shows, and book series that I immediately show her so that she can become as obsessed as I have become. I love that we can just go to each other with whatever we just found and can be as obsessed and insane about the subject as we want. I will always adore and cherish the countless memories of learning every word of Hamilton and playing it constantly in the car. When we finally watched it when it came out on Disney+, my father finally saw how all the songs interlocked with each other because he had been listening to it out of order for months due to us playing in the car for months.

annotations.text: When we finally watched it when it came out on Disney+, my father finally saw how all the songs interlocked with each other because he had been listening to it out of order for months due to us playing in the car for months.
annotations.comment: Is this idea logically connected to the big idea this paragraph is developing?

annotations.context_left: shows, and book series that I immediately show her

paragraphs.body: I feel as though the sense of the bond is bigger than people can imagine. I feel that people who are twins are the luckiest. They have two soulmates in this world. A romantic soulmate and a platonic soulmate also known as their twin. Twins have this sort of built-in soulmate from the very start of their life. They have their person who they will go through life with and who will understand them like no one else. Even though some twins might want to deny that there is a bond and connection between each other and want to run away from it. In my opinion, they cannot run away from it because it is something that sticks with them forever and ever no matter how far away they try to run from it. The bond and relationship they make from such a young age will always catch up to them in the most unexpected way possible.

annotations.text: The bond and relationship they make from such a young age will always catch up to them in the most unexpected way possible.
annotations.comment: this sentence suggests that the next paragraph will explore this idea (which is doesn't, exactly)

annotations.context_left: with and who will understand them like no one else. Even though some twins might want to

paragraphs.body: To describe my bond with my twin is too hard to put into words. I think that every twin will describe their bond differently depending on how they were raised and what life events they went through with each other. For me, I would describe my bond with my twin as something special and something that I will cherish for the rest of my life no matter what. If we are hundreds of miles away, I will always know that I have somebody miles away from me who is cheering for me, and if we are two seconds away. I know that there will be the same amount of support and encouragement as there has always been and will always be. I would also describe the bond as something magical and as something unpredictable. The bond over time will evolve as we evolve as people but that is something that I am looking forward to and cannot wait to continually cheer her on.

Another one of my favorite memories with her is when we randomly go into each other's rooms and start to sing some random song from the million genres of music we both love and adore. It is not just then when we do this. We do this when driving home from work, school, softball, or whatever other adventure we were just on, singing otherwise known as screaming our little hearts out. The songs usually consisted of some old Disney songs, Billy Joel, Mamma Mia, Kansas, The Beatles, Disney original movies songs, early 2000’s songs, and whatever other new artists we just become obsessed with. If you were ever to watch one of our softball practices Claire and I are most likely having full-on performances with each other while everyone looks at us. We always find time to be goofballs and be chaotic little messes around anybody and bless everyone with our beautiful singing and screaming fits. Ashley and I have always had this spontaneous nature between the two of us no matter what. We have always just randomly burst out into song at any point in time. We have always done the randomness and stupidest things when we are bored. We have always talked about the most uncommon topics in any situation. We have always done whatever feels right in the moment even if it was not the most usual decision. We have always had this unpredictable and spontaneous nature that has increased and deepened our bond throughout our lives. And we will continue to do things spontaneously and unpredictably for the rest of our lives together or independently.

annotations.comment: There are lots of engaging details in these first couple of pages, but I wonder if--for the purposes of the essay--you could explain your twin bond more succinctly? Do you need two pages for this big idea?
annotations.context_left: Mamma Mia, Kansas, The Beatles, Disney original movies

paragraphs.body: Claire and I have grown up and become more independent from our family and each other. We have become more independent from our family because in our family it was very common, due to my two older siblings doing it, to go and work at Wegmans for your first job. But this did not happen with us because at the time we were applying to get our first jobs we had softball every weekend which caused our schedules to not be as open as Wegmans would have liked. At the time that we were applying for jobs, we applied to American Eagle and Aerie in the nearby outlets where we grew up. This ended up being the place where we would work and will continue to work there for the foreseeable future. However, we have grown independently through this job with Ashley being scheduled in in American Eagle more and me more in Aerie. At the beginning of our time at American Eagle and Aerie, we both would work on either side but about a month in they started to just schedule her in Eagle and me in Aerie and that is the end of that story. We have developed different skills such as leadership skills and communication skills in our respective stores which are different from each other. Through this job, we both have grown independently in dealing with completely different types of customers and dealing with different overall goals of each store. We have also grown independently from our family in another way by breaking the family tradition of going to Penn State for college. We both decided independently that we wanted to go to Pitt for college because it has better programs for our careers than at Penn State. For Ashley, Pitt has multiple hospitals just on campus for her to do her clinicals when that time comes for her. And for me, Penn State did not have the major that I wanted and Pitt had a great program available for the career. We grew independently from our family and have also created this spontaneous attitude and lifestyle with these decisions. We spontaneously decided to go work somewhere that was not the normal Wegmans job to work. We spontaneously decided to not go to Penn State for college and instead go to Pitt.

annotations.text: each other. We have become more independent from our family because in our family it was very common, due to my two older siblings doing it, to go and work at Wegmans for your first job. paragraphs.body: Ultimately I love just talking to her about anything and everything. All my questions about the randomness that comes into my brain at any point in the day. All the chaoticness that is embedded with those spontaneous inquiries that come out of my mouth at any point. I love it when we drive home from work and talk about the weirdness that goes on working in retail at an outlet. Some of the weirdest experiences that we have experienced is when somebody peed in the fitting rooms and did not tell us, and I walked into the fitting room and saw it. Or when someone just stole a stack of jeans and ran out the door with no care in the world about the consequences. We still do not know what he wanted with female jeggings that were too small for him. Furthermore, when we ended up closing both sides together and going crazy in the back of the store or working Black Friday and trying to convince this man to buy socks because they are “perfect stocking stuffers.” I love living life with her and experiencing all the bizarreness that comes with life.

annotations.text: I love living life with her and experiencing all the bizarreness that comes with life.
annotations.comment: While this may be true (and there are some truly bizarre details in this paragraph :), how is this paragraph related to your essay's central focusing idea? What _is_ your essay's central focusing idea? How does that idea relate to an audience larger than yourself?

paragraphs.body: For our whole lives, we have always done everything together and have always been there for each other and we will continue to be there for each other when we are not physically together. When we grow up and graduate college we will most likely have to separate and go our separate ways. We will have to follow our career paths and that will most likely cause us to be separated from each other. Our careers though somewhat similar but extremely different from each other will cause us to physically separate from each other. She might have to go to a hospital in another state and I might have to go to a hospital or practice in a different state. I might stay here in Pittsburgh and she might go back home and work somewhere around Philly or vice versa.

I know that one day we will be physically separated from each other due to our career paths and the general way that the universe works. I know that that day is coming up faster and faster as time goes on and as we grow up. I’ve known since I was a child that I would not always have my built-in best friend and soulmate with me and that one day we will be physically separated. And that we will have to learn how to navigate our relationship when that hurdle comes up in the future. I know that the relationship that I have with my twin is similar to having a romantic soulmate where we both understand each other like no one else. We also know that we will have each other's back in any situation that we may come across like in a romantic soulmate. In addition, we will have to learn how to get over being far away from each other and having a long-distance relationship with each other. At first, I know it is going to be hard and weird not having her there with me at any given moment. Thought we will persevere and get through it together. I cannot imagine my life without her there at my side but I know that when we see each other it will be even more special than it already is.

annotations.text: works. I know that that day is coming up faster and faster as time goes on and as we grow up. I’ve known since I was a child that I would not always have my built-in best friend and soulmate with me and that one day we will be physically separated.
annotations.comment: Is this related to your essay's center of gravity?

paragraphs.body: Even though the day that I will call “THE SEPARATION” will come and it will ultimately suck and be dreadful I know that it will also be good for us. I know that in the end we both will grow even more and will become more well-rounded people. But for now, I do not want that day to come and I want it to keep getting pushed back farther and farther. I want the day to never exist because I know that once it happens and once we get through that day then it will happen. Then it happened that we are physically separated from each other and not just for a couple of hours or a day. But for a couple of weeks, that will then turn into months. Nevertheless for now I’m going to live in the moment enjoy being physically with my twin and cherish the moments that we will be constantly making with each other for now before that ungodly day happens. Work Cited Pelaez, Sarah. “Why Are Twins so Close?” PsycheSpot, 6 Jan. 2022, www.psychespot.com/mental-awareness/why-are-twins-so-close/.

annotations.text: I know that it will also be good for us.
